pattern.imageEvidence: finished striped lawn with a route-day gate and edge check in progress

geometry.borderRole: status board and route card dividers
geometry.shadowRole: hero media and route board only

pattern.styleContract: Crisp route-day utility with gate-check proof, striped lawn outcome evidence, sun accent actions, and a floating route status board as the signature move.

tagline: Neighborhood routes for lawns that look ready by Friday.

hero.title: GreenStripe route-day lawn care hero image
hero.alt: A lawn care professional checking a wooden gate beside a freshly striped suburban lawn with mower and trimmer ready for route-day service.

contact.serviceArea: Northside, Oak Hill, Westbrook, Maple Bend, and nearby weekly mowing routes with room for one-time cleanup passes when the calendar allows

copy.eyebrow: Weekly mowing routes with gate-check notes
copy.heroText: GreenStripe runs tidy neighborhood routes for mowing, edging, bed touch-ups, and post-visit gate checks, so your yard looks handled before the weekend shows up.
copy.introTitle: A lawn route should feel predictable, not mysterious.
copy.introText: We confirm the access point, set the mowing cadence, trim the edges that make the yard feel finished, and leave simple notes when weather, growth, or cleanup timing changes the plan.
copy.processTitle: How the route stays on track
copy.proofTitle: The small checks make the stripes feel premium
copy.quoteText: Join the route with address, a few yard photos, gate or access notes, and your ideal mowing rhythm. We will reply with the best route fit and a clear first-visit plan.

services.title: Weekly stripe route
services.text: Consistent mowing, clean passes, and route-day timing for lawns that need to look ready before the weekend.

services.title: Edge and bed touch
services.text: Walkways, driveway seams, fence lines, and bed borders get the crisp finish that makes the whole yard look intentional.

services.title: Cleanup reset pass
services.text: Storm sticks, leaf mats, overgrowth, and that one corner nobody wants to talk about get handled before regular mowing starts.

process.title: Check the route fit
process.text: Send yard photos, address, slope or gate notes, and any trouble spots so we can match the right route lane.

process.title: Set the cadence
process.text: We recommend weekly, biweekly, or reset-first service based on growth, shade, edges, and how much polish you want.

process.title: Leave the gate check
process.text: After the visit, we check gates, blow hard surfaces, and flag weather or cleanup notes before the next stop.

proof.label: route planning that protects weekend curb appeal
access and cleanup notes after every visit
weekday quote reply with a route-fit next step

notes: Works: a floating route status board. gives the page a memorable shape.
Proof: finished striped lawn with a route-day gate and edge check in progress. anchors the niche in the first viewport.
Action: Join the route is the main conversion path.

brandBrief.accentBehavior: sun stays reserved for Join the route, active proof cues, and key booking moments.
brandBrief.imageProof: finished striped lawn with a route-day gate and edge check in progress.
brandBrief.avoidLookingLike: Avoid a generic lawn-route-status-board recolor; do not repeat stock hero framing, copied card rhythm, or vague local-service claims.

serviceDetails.turnaround: Friday-ready: route planning that protects weekend curb appeal.
serviceDetails.whatToSend: Send your address or neighborhood, access notes, preferred cadence, photos when useful, and any skip conditions before joining the route.
serviceDetails.prepNote: Send yard photos, address, slope or gate notes, and any trouble spots so we can match the right route lane.
serviceDetails.serviceRhythm: Route-fit flow for Northside, Oak Hill, Westbrook, Maple Bend, and nearby weekly mowing routes with room for one-time cleanup passes when the calendar allows: cadence, access, skip rules, and first-visit notes make the route realistic.
serviceDetails.objectionAnswer: For lawn care service, yard size, gate access, mowing cadence, edging needs, clippings, and weather windows are checked before the first route day is set.
